evals.name: smoke-full-audit-routes-to-methodology-and-fix-prompt
evals.prompt: Audit this marketing site for SEO issues from the codebase, tell me what is broken around canonicals, OG tags, and schema, and then give me a prompt I can hand to another session to fix everything.
evals.expected_output: Uses the methodology, performs a technical and metadata review, and produces a second-session implementation prompt instead of generic SEO advice.

assertions.text: Response starts with repository and page-type analysis rather than framework assumptions
Response covers canonicals, OG tags, and schema distinctly
Response includes a structured handoff prompt for another session

evals.name: edge-case-intentional-noindex-pages-are-not-flagged-as-errors
evals.prompt: Check the repo's SEO, but note that account, cart, checkout, and internal search routes are intentionally noindexed. Focus on real mistakes only.
evals.expected_output: Keeps intentional noindex rules out of the critical findings and focuses on true index-worthy pages.

assertions.text: Response distinguishes intentional exclusions from accidental ones
Response does not recommend indexing account or checkout pages by default

evals.name: negative-link-building-request-does-not-trigger-cleanly
evals.prompt: I need 100 backlink ideas and outreach email copy to raise domain authority fast.
evals.expected_output: Recognizes that this skill is for on-site code and content analysis, not off-page link building or outreach.

assertions.text: Response does not force a codebase SEO audit onto an outreach request
Response identifies off-page SEO as out of scope for this skill

evals.name: disclosure-social-preview-question-loads-preview-reference
evals.prompt: Why do link previews break even though the title tag looks fine? Check OG tags, twitter cards, favicon, and preview image behavior.
evals.expected_output: Loads the metadata and previews reference and separates search metadata from social metadata.

assertions.text: Response checks OG tags and social card fields directly
Response explains that search metadata and social metadata are related but distinct
